back_translation: The sons of the prophets said to Elisha, “Behold, the place where we meet with you is too small for us.
Let us go to the Jordan, where each of us can get a log, so we can build ourselves a place to live there.” Elisha said, “Go.”
One of them said, “Please go with your servants.” He answered, “I will go.”
So Elisha went with them, and when they came to the Jordan they began cutting down trees.
As one of them was felling a tree, the iron axe head fell into the water, and he cried out, “Oh, my master! It was borrowed!”
The man of God asked, “Where did it fall?” When he showed him the place, the man of God cut a stick, threw it there, and made the iron float.
Elisha said, “Lift it out,” and the man reached out his hand and took it.
Now the king of Aram was at war with Israel. After consulting his servants, he said, “I will set my camp at such and such a place.”
The man of God sent word to the king of Israel, “Beware of passing this place, for the Arameans are going down there.”
So the king of Israel sent to the place the man of God had warned him about. Time and again Elisha warned the king, so he was on guard in those places.
This enraged the king of Aram, and he called his servants and asked, “Tell me, which of us is on the side of the king of Israel?”
One of his servants said, “No one, my lord the king; but Elisha the prophet in Israel tells the king of Israel even the words you speak in your bedroom.”
The king said, “Go and see where he is, that I may send men to seize him.” And he was told, “Elisha is in Dothan.”
So the king of Aram sent horses, chariots, and a great army; they came by night and surrounded the city.
When the servant of the man of God rose early and went out, behold, an army with horses and chariots had surrounded the city. So he said to Elisha, “Oh, my master, what shall we do?”
Elisha answered, “Do not be afraid, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them.”
Then Elisha prayed, “O LORD, please open his eyes that he may see.” And the LORD opened the servant’s eyes, and he saw that the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha.
As the Arameans came down against him, Elisha prayed to the LORD, “Please strike these people with blindness.” And He struck them with blindness, according to the word of Elisha.
Elisha told them, “This is not the way, and this is not the city. Follow me, and I will bring you to the man you seek.” And he led them to Samaria.
When they entered Samaria, Elisha said, “O LORD, open the eyes of these men that they may see.” The LORD opened their eyes, and they looked and found themselves in the midst of Samaria.
When the king of Israel saw them, he asked Elisha, “My father, shall I kill them? Shall I kill them?”
Elisha answered, “Do not kill them. Would you kill those you have captured with your sword and bow? Set food and water before them, that they may eat and drink and return to their master.”
So the king made a great feast for them, and after they had eaten and drunk, he sent them away, and they returned to their master. And the Aramean raiders did not come into the land of Israel again.
Some time later, Ben-hadad king of Aram mustered his whole army and went up and besieged Samaria.
There was a great famine in Samaria; the siege lasted so long that a donkey’s head sold for eighty shekels of silver, and a quarter cab of dove’s dung for five shekels of silver.
As the king of Israel was passing by on the wall, a woman cried out to him, “Help, my lord the king!”
He answered, “If the LORD does not help you, from where can I find help for you—from the threshing floor or the winepress?”
Then the king asked her, “What is the matter?” She answered, “This woman said to me, ‘Give up your son, that we may eat him today, and tomorrow we will eat my son.’
So we boiled my son and ate him. The next day I said to her, ‘Give up your son, that we may eat him,’ but she had hidden her son.”
When the king heard the woman’s words, he tore his clothes; and as he passed by on the wall, the people saw the sackcloth he wore underneath, next to his skin.
He declared, “May God punish me severely if the head of Elisha son of Shaphat remains on his shoulders today!”

Now Elisha was sitting in his house, and the elders were sitting with him. The king sent a man ahead, but before the messenger arrived, Elisha said to the elders, “Do you see how this son of a murderer has sent to cut off my head? Look, when the messenger comes, shut the door and hold it against him. Is not the sound of his master’s feet behind him?”
While he was still speaking with them, the messenger came down to him; and the king said, “This calamity is from the LORD. Why should I wait for the LORD any longer?”
